text:
It’s become something of a tradition for Olympians to get inked up with the iconic rings to commemorate their participation at the Games – but Team GB superstar Keely Hodgkinson will not be joining that club thissummer.

Hodgkinson lived up to her billing as the pre-Games favourite for the women’s 800m with a sensational performance in last night’s pulsating final,blowing her rivals away to clinch gold in emphatic fashionin Paris.

The 22-year-old’s masterful showing at the Stade de France came three years on from the silver she won as a teenager at the previousOlympic Gamesin Tokyo.

Hodgkinson’s latest victory saw her become only the tenth British woman to win an athletics Olympic gold and just the third female Brit in history to clinch 800m gold, following in the footsteps ofDame Kelly Holmesand Ann Packer.

Follow Metro.co.uk’s live blog for updates from the Paris Olympics 2024

Ethiopia’s Tsige Dguma and Kenya’s Mary Moraa both pushed Hodgkinson hard in the final 200 metres of the race but ultimately had to settle for silver and bronze respectively.

After claiming silver back in 2021, Hodgkinson decided to remember the moment by getting a tattoo of the word ‘Tokyo’ on her left arm, written in Japanese.

‘I have Tokyo, obviously for the Olympics. I felt like, for me, it’s a bit more subtle than getting the rings but it means something as well. That’s my favourite [tattoo],’ she recently told Sky Sports when asked for the story behind her many tattoos.

In a separate interview withActivate the Burners, Hodgkinson went into further detail about her Tokyo tattoo before explaining why she is never likely to get branded with the Olympic rings.

‘This was my spontaneous tattoo on Oxford Street when I got back from Tokyo, so it says Tokyo,’ she said.

‘Personally, no hate to anyone, I don’t like the Olympic rings. Like, I do like them but not on me.’

What an incredible moment for Keely Hodgkinson ð¥¹#Paris2024#Olympics#BBCOlympicspic.twitter.com/CowXsXDFjF

Asked if she was tempted to get the Olympic rings tattooed, Hodgkinson replied: ‘No, I won’t get them!

‘I think they’re really ugly. No, I just don’t like them as a tattoo for me.

‘I just don’t think it would look right on me so I thought this [her Tokyo tattoo] was a bit more low-key.’

On her right arm, Hodgkinson has a tattoo paying tribute to her late grandparents and another nodding to her star sign, Pisces, which she got aged 18 and ‘kind of regrets’.

‘These are for my grandparents who passed away,’ she explained.

‘This is my star sign… I kind of regret this one though… I was 18.

‘And then this one is matching with my best friend. I got all these when I was like 18 so don’t judge me!

After her remarkable win in the French capital, a near-speechless Hodgkinson said she simply ‘could not believe’ that she had achieved her life-long dream by claiming gold.

That was absolutely incredible. I’ve worked so hard for this over the last year and I think you could see how much it meant to me when I crossed the line,’ Hodgkinson told BBC Sport.

‘I can’t believe I’ve finally done it and to do it here… where better. The audience was absolutely incredible, it felt like a home crowd in Paris. I’m super, super happy.’

title: Why Team GB star Keely Hodgkinson would never get Olympic rings tattoo | Metro News
description: It's become tradition for athletes to get inked up with an Olympic rings tattoo - but Keely Hodgkinson will not be joining that club.
